title: Frame embeddings and search index
analysis: Create autopilot/analyze/embeddings.py implementing four functions: compute_embeddings (SigLIP 2 ViT-SO400M/14 at 0.5 FPS), build_search_index (FAISS IVF), search_by_text, and search_by_image. The codebase has strong established patterns from objects.py, asr.py, and faces.py: TYPE_CHECKING imports for GPUScheduler/ModelConfig/CatalogDB, custom error class, idempotency checks, lazy heavy-module imports (cv2, transformers, faiss), structured logging, and transactional DB writes via context manager. CatalogDB already has batch_insert_embeddings(), get_embeddings_for_media(), get_all_clip_embeddings(), and the clip_embeddings table schema (media_id, frame_number, embedding BLOB). ModelConfig already has clip_model='google/siglip2-so400m-patch14'. tests/test_embeddings.py exists with 3 DB helper tests but they have bugs (wrong insert_media signature using nonexistent kwargs like filename, filepath, file_hash, width, height — actual signature is insert_media(id, file_path, **kwargs)). These must be fixed before extending the file. The mocking strategy follows test_objects.py: mock cv2.VideoCapture, mock the ML model (SigLIP via transformers instead of YOLO), use real in-memory CatalogDB. For FAISS, use faiss-cpu in tests for real index behavior. The index metadata mapping (FAISS index position -> media_id, frame_number) will be stored as a JSON sidecar file alongside the .faiss index file. autopilot/analyze/embeddings.py does NOT yet exist and must be created from scratch.

prerequisites.description: Fix existing test_embeddings.py TestDBHelpers tests: replace incorrect insert_media kwargs (filename, filepath, file_hash, width, height) with correct call matching CatalogDB.insert_media(id, file_path, **optional_kwargs). E.g., catalog_db.insert_media('vid1', '/v1.mp4'). The get_all_clip_embeddings() method already exists in db.py. Verify all 3 DB helper tests pass.

steps.description: Write TestPublicAPI class in test_embeddings.py: (1) test_embedding_error_importable - EmbeddingError importable and is Exception subclass; (2) test_compute_embeddings_signature - importable with positional params (media_id, video_path, db, scheduler, config) and keyword-only sample_fps, batch_size; (3) test_build_search_index_signature - importable with params (db, output_path); (4) test_search_by_text_signature - importable with params (query, index_path, model, top_k=10); (5) test_search_by_image_signature - importable with params (image, index_path, model, top_k=10). Verify all are in __all__.

Create autopilot/analyze/embeddings.py with module skeleton: docstring, from __future__ import annotations, TYPE_CHECKING imports for GPUScheduler/ModelConfig/CatalogDB, EmbeddingError class, module-level logger, __all__ exports ['EmbeddingError', 'compute_embeddings', 'build_search_index', 'search_by_text', 'search_by_image'], function stubs with correct signatures returning None/empty-list. Follow objects.py module pattern.

Write TestComputeSampleIndices class for _compute_sample_indices helper: (1) test_half_fps_30fps - 300 frames at 30fps at 0.5 sample_fps -> [0, 60, 120, 180, 240]; (2) test_half_fps_24fps - 240 frames at 24fps -> [0, 48, 96, 144, 192]; (3) test_empty_video - 0 frames -> []; (4) test_short_video - 30 frames at 30fps -> [0]; (5) test_low_fps_video - 10 frames at 1fps at 0.5 sample_fps -> [0, 2, 4, 6, 8]; (6) test_custom_sample_fps - 300 frames at 30fps at 1.0 sample_fps -> [0, 30, 60, ..., 270].

Implement _compute_sample_indices(total_frames, fps, sample_fps=0.5) -> list[int] helper: interval = max(1, int(fps / sample_fps)), return list(range(0, total_frames, interval)). Handle total_frames <= 0 -> [].

Write TestIdempotency class: (1) test_existing_embeddings_skips - insert media + clip embedding rows into DB, call compute_embeddings, verify scheduler.model not called; (2) test_no_existing_embeddings_proceeds - insert media only, call compute_embeddings with full mocks (cv2, SigLIP model, scheduler), verify scheduler.model IS called. Include mock helper functions _make_mock_cv2(), _make_mock_capture() following test_objects.py patterns, plus _make_mock_siglip() helper returning (model, processor) tuple where model.get_image_features() returns deterministic 768-d tensor.

Implement compute_embeddings: idempotency check via db.get_embeddings_for_media(), video path validation (path.exists() raising EmbeddingError), cv2.VideoCapture open check (isOpened() raising EmbeddingError), basic structure with lazy cv2 import, try/finally with cap.release().

Write TestErrorHandling class: (1) test_video_not_found_raises - nonexistent path raises EmbeddingError matching 'not found'; (2) test_video_not_opened_raises - cap.isOpened() False raises EmbeddingError matching 'Failed to open'; (3) test_frame_read_failure_skipped - one frame read fails via cap.read()=(False,None), other frames still produce embeddings in DB.

Wire up error handling paths in compute_embeddings to pass error tests. Ensure path.exists() check is before lazy cv2 import, cap.isOpened() checked after VideoCapture, frame read failures logged as WARNING and skipped.

Write TestEmbeddingComputation class: (1) test_correct_embedding_count - 300 frames at 30fps stores 5 embeddings (0.5 FPS); (2) test_embedding_size_768d - each stored BLOB is 3072 bytes (768*4 float32); (3) test_embedding_roundtrip - stored embedding recoverable via np.frombuffer float32 matches original values; (4) test_model_called_with_clip_model - scheduler.model called with config.clip_model; (5) test_correct_frame_numbers - DB rows have frame_numbers [0, 60, 120, 180, 240]; (6) test_batch_size_parameter - different batch_size produces same correct result. Mock SigLIP as (model, processor) tuple: processor returns mock tensor dict, model.get_image_features returns mock 768-d output with .cpu().numpy() chain.

Implement compute_embeddings core loop: load (model, processor) via scheduler.model(config.clip_model), sample frames with _compute_sample_indices, read frames via cv2, convert BGR->RGB via PIL.Image.fromarray, preprocess via processor(images=pil_image, return_tensors='pt'), encode via model.get_image_features(**inputs), L2-normalize via F.normalize or manual norm, detach().cpu().numpy(), pack float32 to bytes via .tobytes(), accumulate rows, batch_insert_embeddings in with-db block. Use lazy imports for torch and PIL.

Write TestLogging class: (1) test_log_contains_media_id_on_start - INFO log includes media_id; (2) test_log_contains_completion - INFO log includes 'Completed' and embedding count; (3) test_log_skipping_on_idempotent - INFO log mentions 'skipping' when embeddings already exist; (4) test_log_warning_on_frame_failure - WARNING logged when frame read fails.

Add structured logging to compute_embeddings: start message with media_id/total_frames/fps/model_name, completion message with embedding count, skip message on idempotent early return, warning on frame read failure. logger = logging.getLogger(__name__).

Write TestBuildSearchIndex class: (1) test_empty_db_no_index - empty DB returns without creating files; (2) test_small_dataset_creates_index - insert 10 mock 768-d embeddings, build index, output_path file exists; (3) test_metadata_mapping_written - sidecar .ids.json exists with correct [[media_id, frame_number], ...] entries; (4) test_index_vector_count - loaded FAISS index.ntotal matches embedding count; (5) test_index_dimension - index.d equals 768; (6) test_large_dataset_uses_ivf - for n>=256 embeddings, index is IVF type. Use real faiss-cpu for these tests.

Implement build_search_index: lazy import faiss/json. Load via db.get_all_clip_embeddings(), stack numpy float32 matrix, L2-normalize. If n<256: IndexFlatIP. If n>=256: IndexIVFFlat(quantizer=IndexFlatIP(768), 768, nlist=max(1,int(sqrt(n))), METRIC_INNER_PRODUCT) -> train -> add. faiss.write_index to output_path. Write .ids.json sidecar mapping. Empty DB -> INFO log + return.

Write TestSearchByText class: (1) test_returns_correct_tuple_types - each result is (str, int, float); (2) test_top_k_limits_results - top_k=3 returns at most 3; (3) test_text_encoder_called - model.get_text_features invoked; (4) test_results_contain_valid_media_ids - all media_ids from indexed data. Build small real FAISS index in tmp_path fixture, mock SigLIP text encoder.

Implement search_by_text: lazy import faiss. Load FAISS index via faiss.read_index(str(index_path)). Load .ids.json mapping. Unpack (model, processor) from model param. Encode: processor(text=[query], return_tensors='pt', padding=True) -> model.get_text_features() -> L2-normalize -> numpy float32. index.search(query_vec, top_k). Map results through sidecar. Return list of (media_id, frame_number, distance) tuples.

Write TestSearchByImage class: (1) test_returns_correct_tuple_types - each result is (str, int, float); (2) test_top_k_limits_results - top_k=3 returns at most 3; (3) test_image_encoder_called - model.get_image_features invoked; (4) test_numpy_image_accepted - accepts np.ndarray. Build real FAISS index in tmp_path, mock SigLIP vision encoder.

Implement search_by_image: extract shared _load_index_and_mapping(index_path) helper. Convert np.ndarray to PIL Image (RGB). Encode via processor(images=pil_image, return_tensors='pt') -> model.get_image_features() -> L2-normalize -> numpy. Search + map. Return tuples. Refactor search_by_text to use same _load_index_and_mapping helper.

Write TestIntegration class: test_compute_build_search_pipeline - (1) compute_embeddings on mock video stores 5 rows in DB; (2) build_search_index creates index file + mapping with 5 entries; (3) search_by_text returns results with valid media_id/frame_number from original data; (4) search_by_image returns results; (5) frame_numbers in results match original sample indices. Use real CatalogDB + real faiss-cpu, mock cv2 and SigLIP model consistently throughout.

Final verification: run full test suite, fix edge cases. Ensure cap.release() in finally, proper error wrapping, clean docstrings (Google-style Args/Returns), type hints, __all__ exports correct. Module follows all codebase conventions.

design_decisions.decision: Use inner product (IndexFlatIP/IndexIVFFlat with METRIC_INNER_PRODUCT) for FAISS since SigLIP embeddings are L2-normalized
design_decisions.rationale: For L2-normalized vectors, inner product equals cosine similarity and is faster than L2 distance. SigLIP produces normalized embeddings, so IP gives correct semantic similarity ranking. Higher IP score = more similar.

design_decisions.decision: Store FAISS index metadata as a JSON sidecar file (.ids.json) alongside the index
design_decisions.rationale: FAISS indices only store integer IDs. A JSON file mapping FAISS row index -> [media_id, frame_number] is simple, human-readable, and avoids pickle security concerns. The mapping list is small (~90K entries for 50h at 0.5 FPS = ~3.6MB JSON).

design_decisions.decision: Two-tier FAISS index strategy: IndexFlatIP (<256 vectors), IndexIVFFlat (>=256 vectors)
design_decisions.rationale: Small datasets don't need IVF overhead - exact brute-force search is fast. Medium-to-large datasets benefit from IVF partitioning with nlist=sqrt(n). The PRD example (50h at 0.5 FPS = 90K) falls in the IVFFlat range. IndexIVFPQ can be added later for >1M vectors.

design_decisions.decision: Frame sampling at 0.5 FPS via _compute_sample_indices helper with interval = max(1, int(fps / sample_fps))
design_decisions.rationale: Follows pattern of _compute_frame_indices in objects.py. Extractable helper makes it independently testable. 0.5 FPS = 1 frame every 2 seconds. max(1,...) prevents zero interval for very low FPS sources.

design_decisions.decision: GPU scheduler model entry stores (model, processor) tuple for SigLIP
design_decisions.rationale: SigLIP requires both model and processor for inference. Storing them together in the scheduler means a single model() context manager call provides everything needed. The caller unpacks: with scheduler.model(name) as (model, processor).

design_decisions.decision: search_by_text/search_by_image take pre-loaded (model, processor) tuple rather than scheduler
design_decisions.rationale: Per task spec, search functions accept a 'model' parameter directly. Search may be called interactively many times - the caller manages model lifecycle. This decouples search from the GPU scheduler's batch processing context.

design_decisions.decision: L2-normalize embeddings at compute time before storage and at search time for queries
design_decisions.rationale: Pre-normalizing stored embeddings avoids redundant computation during index building. Query vectors are also normalized at search time. FAISS IndexFlatIP on normalized vectors gives cosine similarity directly.

design_decisions.decision: Expose sample_fps and batch_size as keyword-only parameters on compute_embeddings
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the pattern from detect_objects (batch_size, sparse as keyword-only). Allows callers to override the 0.5 FPS default for different use cases while keeping the common case simple.

reuse.how: Existing method accepts (media_id, frame_number, embedding_blob) tuples. Use directly in compute_embeddings to store SigLIP embeddings.
Use for idempotency check in compute_embeddings - if media already has embeddings, skip processing.
Already exists. Used by build_search_index to load all embeddings for FAISS index construction.
Table already exists with (media_id TEXT, frame_number INTEGER, embedding BLOB) PK. No schema changes needed.
Use scheduler.model(config.clip_model) to load/unload SigLIP model with VRAM management, same pattern as objects.py/asr.py/faces.py.
Default value 'google/siglip2-so400m-patch14' already configured. Access via config.clip_model.
Reuse for all embedding tests. Fresh in-memory DB per test with full schema.
Replicate: mock CAP_PROP constants, configurable fps/total_frames, cap.read() returns (True, frame). Use patch.dict(sys.modules, {'cv2': mock_cv2}).
MagicMock scheduler with model() returning context manager (__enter__=mock_model, __exit__=False). Access: scheduler.model.return_value.__enter__ = MagicMock(return_value=mock_model).
Follow identical layout: from __future__ import annotations, TYPE_CHECKING guard, __all__, logger, custom error class, lazy heavy imports inside functions, try/finally for resource cleanup.
